ucs.title: [LLM] Group5 (suspected Iranian) Syrian-themed lure → njRAT/NanoCore install chain (commodity RAT persistence after Office/script spawn)
ucs.description: Hunts the Group5 delivery-to-install pattern: Syrian/Iranian-themed spearphish or watering-hole drop produces an Office/script parent that drops an njRAT or NanoCore payload into %AppData%/%ProgramData% and registers HKCU Run-key persistence within minutes. This combination — commodity RAT staging + auto-run from a user-writable path immediately after a document/script execution — is signature Group5 tradecraft (Citizen Lab 'Group5', MITRE G0043).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","hh.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count values(Registry.registry_value_data) as run_value from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*" Registry.registry_value_data IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Roaming\\*","*\\Public\\*") by Registry.dest Registry.registry_value_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` ] | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Temp\\*") Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.exe","*.scr","*.dat","run.dat") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name ] | where lastTime - firstTime < 1800 | eval risk="njRAT/NanoCore install chain - Group5 (G0043)" | table firstTime dest user parent_proc process_name cmdline registry_value_name run_value file_name risk
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 1d;
let officeKids = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","hh.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe")
| project DeviceId, ChildTs=Timestamp, ParentProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProc=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine, AccountName;
let runKeys = DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
| where RegistryValueData has_any ("AppData","Roaming","ProgramData","Temp","Public")
| project DeviceId, RegTs=Timestamp, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData;
let drops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\")
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".dat" or FileName =~ "run.dat"
| project DeviceId, DropTs=Timestamp, DropPath=FolderPath, DropName=FileName, DropSha256=SHA256;
officeKids
| join kind=inner runKeys on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', ChildTs, RegTs)) <= 30
| join kind=inner drops on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', ChildTs, DropTs)) <= 30
| project ChildTs, DeviceId, AccountName, ParentProc, ChildProc, ChildCmd, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, DropPath, DropName, DropSha256
| extend ThreatActor="Group5 (G0043) - suspected Iranian nexus", Malware="njRAT / NanoCore"
ucs.rationale: Group5's reported intrusions consistently chain a maldoc/script lure with installation of off-the-shelf RATs (njRAT, NanoCore) that drop into user-writable paths and register HKCU Run-key autoruns within the same session. Generic 'Office spawns PowerShell' or 'Run-key created' rules each fire too often alone; requiring all three artefacts (suspicious Office/script child + Run-key pointing at a use

ucs.title: [LLM] Group5 surveillance burst: keylog + screencapture artefacts followed by self-delete (T1056.001 + T1113 + T1070.004)
ucs.description: Hunts the Group5 post-install surveillance loop characteristic of njRAT/NanoCore tasking against Syrian-opposition targets: a single non-system process writes a keylog/clipboard cache file and a screenshot file under %AppData%/%Temp%, then deletes its own staged artefacts to defeat forensics. This three-technique combo on one process is the on-host fingerprint of Group5's RAT operators (G0043).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_name) as files values(Filesystem.action) as actions from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.user _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval has_keylog=if(match(mvjoin(files,","),"(?i)(keylog|klog|\.log|clip\.txt|keys\.txt)"),1,0) | eval has_screen=if(match(mvjoin(files,","),"(?i)(screen|scr_|capture|\.bmp|\.jpg|\.png)"),1,0) | eval has_delete=if(match(mvjoin(actions,","),"(?i)delete"),1,0) | where has_keylog=1 AND has_screen=1 AND has_delete=1 | where NOT match(process_name,"(?i)(onedrive|teams|chrome|msedge|firefox|explorer|searchapp|snippingtool|screensketch)\.exe") | eval risk="Group5 (G0043) RAT surveillance loop - njRAT/NanoCore tasking" | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name files actions risk
ucs.kql:
let win = 30m;
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("onedrive.exe","teams.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","explorer.exe","searchapp.exe","snippingtool.exe","screensketch.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe")
| extend isKeylog = (FileName matches regex @"(?i)(keylog|klog|clip\.txt|keys\.txt|\.log)$"),
         isScreen = (FileName matches regex @"(?i)(screen|scr_|capture).*\.(bmp|jpe?g|png)$" or FileName endswith ".bmp"),
         isDelete = (ActionType == "FileDeleted")
| summarize keylogs = countif(isKeylog),
            screens = countif(isScreen),
            deletes = countif(isDelete),
            sampleFiles = make_set(FileName, 25),
            firstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            lastSeen  = max(Timestamp)
  by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessAccountName, bin(Timestamp, win)
| where keylogs > 0 and screens > 0 and deletes > 0
| extend ThreatActor="Group5 (G0043)", Malware="njRAT / NanoCore (commodity)", Note="Single process exhibits keylog + screencap + self-delete within 30m"
| project firstSeen, lastSeen, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, keylogs, screens, deletes, sampleFiles, ThreatActor, Malware, Note
ucs.rationale: Group5's MITRE-documented post-compromise behaviours are exactly T1056.001 (keylogging), T1113 (screen capture) and T1070.004 (file deletion) — the three-in-one surveillance/anti-forensics behaviour typical of njRAT and NanoCore plugins. Looking for a single non-browser/non-collab process producing keylog-named files AND screenshot-named files AND deleting files in user-writable directories within
